mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.12% as of 2009-01-15 indicates tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 3.8% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in demand destruction.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 3.8%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1200k units signals synchronized demand collapse across consumer and construction sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to earnings forecast revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 696354M and HOUST at 1434k as of 2009-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction in consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference > 0.02 in favor of single-series model).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 101.5 index points signals contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness, reducing corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings multiples downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 101.5, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declining month-over-month combined with rising unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity markets forward-price earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines >200k MoM AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156342k and UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2009-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond UNRATE alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1300k units signals contraction in residential investment and forward-looking construction demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings for cyclical sectors dependent on housing activity.
regime_hint: housing market contraction and residential investment collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1300k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month below 304.0 combined with federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening in a deflationary environment, creating real rate compression that reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 304.348 and FEDFUNDS at 5.12% as of 2009-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal underperforms a simple FEDFUNDS-only model (Rank IC difference > 0.02 in favor of univariate).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerating below 2.0% annualized rate signals weakening aggregate demand and economic growth momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice earnings growth expectations downward in response to slowing macroeconomic activity.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20404.088 as of 2008-10-15 indicates recent economic weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
